text: The performance of machine learning algorithms is highly susceptible to the adverse effects of large numbers of redundant and irrelevant features in the data. An increase in the number of features results in a dramatic decrease in the accuracy of the machine learning model. In this work, we propose IGRF-RFE, an Integrated Gradient-RF Feature Reduction and Elimination approach for multi-class network anomaly detection by using a multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. The proposed approach exploits the computational advantages of filter based feature selection methods along with the improved accuracy of wrapper based feature selection techniques to remove irrelevant and redundant features from the data and to achieve higher classification accuracy.
